description_long: This church features a tall, pointed steeple with a spire, rising above a rectangular nave and a semi-cylindrical apse at the rear. The facade is adorned with a large arched entrance and a tall, narrow window above, framed by simple architectural detailing. The side walls display arched windows with what appear to be wooden shutters, and the roof is pitched with a reddish-brown color. The structure includes a secondary, smaller annex with a sloped roof attached to one side. The exterior is primarily finished in white with subtle beige trim and a red base, giving it a clean and classic appearance. The overall design suggests a modest, traditional church, likely influenced by Gothic Revival or Neo-Gothic architectural styles.
description: A traditional white church with a tall steeple, arched windows, and a red-brown pitched roof, featuring a semi-cylindrical apse and a side annex.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with an arched doorway, tall steeple, and a large window above the door.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the arched windows with shutters, the length of the nave, and the pitched roof.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the semi-cylindrical apse and the attached side annex with a sloped roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing more arched windows, the annex, and the steeple with contrasting dark paneling.
